                       Joseph M. Finerty, Editor

                                    Editors' Note

                                        Beginning with this issue, table C-10, Indexes of output per man-hour, hourly compensation,
                                    unit costs, and prices, private economy, seasonally adjusted (page 98) has been expanded to in-
                                    clude data on manufacturing and on nonfinancial corporations, A new table has also been added:
                                    C-11, Percent changes from preceding quarter and years in output per man-hour, hourly compen-
                                    sation, unit costs, and prices, private economy, seasonally adjusted at annual rate (page 99).

                                                                                CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                                           In addition t o the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                                           Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below:

                                                                                         Jan.   Feb.   Mar.   Apr.   May   July   Aug. Sept.   Oct.   Nov.

                                    Annual averages                                       x

                                     current seasonal factors                                                                                   (1)
                                    State and area annual averages                                                    X

                                    Area definitions                                                                  X


                                          The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The
                                    October 1972 issue marks the introduction of March 1971 benchmarks.

                                                                                       ance with the Standard Industrial Classification Manual, Bureau

                 Federal-State cooperation                                                 Employment data, except those for the Federal Government,

                     Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the
                                                                                       any part of the pay period which includes the 12th of the
                 respondent fills out a single employment or job vacancy-labor

                 turnover reporting form, which is then used for national, State,      figures represent the number of persons who occupied positions

                     Two types of data collection schedules are used: Form BLS         employed are persons who are laid off, on leave without
                 790—Monthly Report on Employment, Payroll, and Hours;                 pay, or on strike for the entire period or who are hired but
                 and Form DL 1219-Monthly Report on Job Openings and                   have not been paid during the period.
                 Labor Turnover. These schedules are of the "shuttle" type,

                 agency returns the schedule to the respondent each month so
                 that the next month's data can be entered. This procedure                 Industry hours and earnings

                                                         Unemployment Insurance Data

                     Insured unemployment represents the number of persons               as a percent of average covered employment in a 12-month
                 reporting a week of unemployment under an unemployment in-              period ending 6 to 8 months prior to the week of reference.
                 surance program. It includes some persons who are working part          Initial claims are notices filed by those losing jobs covered by an
                 time who would be counted as employed in the payroll and                unemployment insurance program that they are starting a
                 household surveys. Excluded are persons who have exhausted              period of unemployment. A claimant who continued to be
                 their benefit rights and workers who have not earned rights to          unemployed a full week is then counted in the insured un-
                 unemployment insurance. In general, excluded from coverage              employment figure.
                 are those persons engaged in agriculture, domestic service, un-
                 paid family work, selected nonprofit organizations, State and              Because of differences in State laws and procedures under
                 local government and self-employment. Also excluded from                which unemployment insurance programs are operated, State un-
                 the insured unemployment count, but included as employed in             employment rates generally indicate, but do not precisely mea-
                 the household survey, are those persons who earned no wages             ure, differences among the individual States. Persons wishing to
                 during the payroll period because they were temporarily absent          receive a detailed description of the nature, sources, inclusions
                 from their jobs due to taking time off, illness and industrial          and exclusions, and limitations of unemployment insurance data
                 dispute as well as unpaid vacations. The rate of insured un-            should address their inquiries to Manpower Administration,
